prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `3bd292e8`.
codex_response:
Implemented the ARC2 package for `3bd292e8`. The rule is: treat each connected `7` region as a node, connect two regions when the same `2` barrier cell touches both, seed the bottom-left `7` region as `5`, then alternate `5/3` by BFS parity across that region graph. The verifier is in [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_3bd292e8/verifier.py#L24), the generator is in [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_3bd292e8/generator.py#L139), and the package wiring is in [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_3bd292e8/__init__.py#L1).

I used the `arc2_opus46_summary.json` hint and discarded the corner-based part of the sonnet hint; that note is recorded in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_3bd292e8/notes.md#L1). No new shared primitive was needed. The generator explicitly builds 1-3 disconnected monotone red paths from the top/left border to the bottom/right border, checks that each one splits the orange background into a new region, and then derives the output with the same parity-coloring rule.

Validation passed end-to-end: official verifier `4/4`, generated verifier `1000/1000`, and I visually checked the preview sheets. Artifacts are at [task.json](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/3bd292e8/task.json), [originals.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/3bd292e8/previews/originals.png), [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/3bd292e8/previews/preview_01.png), [preview_02.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/3bd292e8/previews/preview_02.png), and [preview_03.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/3bd292e8/previews/preview_03.png).
